title: Meet the VCs hunting for AI-native social,  gaming, and dating apps
byline: Sydney Bradley, Geoff Weiss, Henry Chandonnet
excerpt: From dating apps to new takes on the social network for the AI era, meet 22 VC firms backing innovative internet startups.
content_html:
Meet 22 VCs on the hunt for the next breakout startup in social media

By Sydney Bradley

Something has to change, right?

Instagram, Snapchat, and TikTok have dominated the social media landscape for years. As AI infiltrates every platform — and many corners of our lives — new social media founders, apps, and internet behaviors are emerging.

From vibe coding to cultural shifts shaping how people want to connect, it's a perfect storm for founders to experiment with social media.

AI has "essentially brought down the cost of making anything," Amy Wu Martin, a partner at Menlo Ventures, told Business Insider.

Menlo is an investor in Anthropic, whose product Claude Code has radically changed the product-building pipeline for startups.

"If you want to build a consumer AI company, you may not need a lot of engineers, but you still need cash for Anthropic credits," said Vanessa Larco, who cofounded Premise, a new VC firm backing AI-native startups.

While it's easier than ever to make an app out of thin air, it's difficult to raise venture capital for a consumer-facing social media upstart.

PitchBook data on social startup investments shows that after a peak in 2021 (239 deals totaling $2.2 billion), the number of deals has declined each year. In 2025, PitchBook counted 97 deals totaling $330 million.

Still, there's a cohort of investors with hungry eyes searching for what will stick.

Business Insider is highlighting investors at 22 VC firms that are actively funding or scouting out innovative social startups. Our third annual list is sourced from nominations by startup founders and venture peers, as well as our own reporting.

These investors, across the board, are excited by what AI has unlocked for the category, and many are bullish on consumer investments even as the broader VC space shies away from it. Our list includes big-shot firms that backed many of the mobile-era giants, as well as AI-era funds founded by VCs who wanted to take things into their own hands.

They're backing startups building agentic solutions for our personal relationships, companies focused on getting us off our phones and into real life, and vibe-coding startups turning software into content.

As Nicole Quinn — a former investor at Lightspeed Venture Partners who left in 2025 to cofound Connect Ventures — put it, funding this next wave of internet startups will largely be investing in the "future of joy."

Meet the investors at 22 VC firms on the hunt for startups changing how we connect online:

A16z has an accelerator that backs many early-stage internet startups

Investors: Andrew Chen, general partner; Olivia Moore, partner; Justine Moore, partner

Andreessen Horowitz backed many of the biggest hits of the mobile era, including Instagram, Pinterest, Reddit, and Roblox. It's also backed a slew of social companies from the 2020s, like BeReal, Clubhouse, Partiful, and Substack.

The firm has been actively backing consumer applications of AI, with investors including the twin-investor duo Olivia and Justine Moore.

A16z also runs a competitive accelerator, Speedrun, which invests up to $1 million in early-stage consumer startups. The accelerator is spearheaded by general partner Andrew Chen.

Recent investments:

  • Town: An AI agent service for managing inboxes and meetings
  • Sekai: A platform that creates interactive mini-apps from text prompts
  • Sitch: An AI-powered dating app
  • Wabi: A platform for creating mini-apps without any coding

Babylon is eyeing fresh startups with a clear impact on culture

Investor: Hugo Amsellem, cofounder and general partner

Amsellem is a French investor who recently moved to San Francisco. He rebranded the firm from Intuition VC to Babylon in 2026, focusing the fund on startups that "move culture."

Before becoming a VC, he worked at creator economy startup Jellysmack, overseeing M&A strategy. Babylon has backed a string of early-stage consumer social startups, including music social app Airbuds, meme and GIF platform Klipy, and AI social startup Eigen.

He's eyeing startups building new takes on social networking powered by AI, such as Eigen. The social startup is building a "mutual friend" layer for AI, meaning a way to connect you with people and share bits of information, like a friend might in real life.

"That's the type of thing that AI will enable," Amsellem said, supercharging "those existing human behaviors without actual humans in the middle."

Babylon typically writes checks up to $250,000 for pre-seed rounds.

Recent investments:

  • Eigen: an AI social startup building a "mutual friend" to connect people
  • Miso: an agentic travel app
  • Delphi: an AI twin startup

Creator Ventures has 'creator' in its name, but it's doubled down on consumer internet startups

Investor: Sasha Kaletsky, cofounder and managing partner

Kaletsky cofounded Creator Ventures with YouTuber Caspar Lee in the UK and is now based in New York. Focused on early-stage consumer internet startups, the firm closed Fund II at $45 million in 2025.

Creator Ventures has invested in startups looking to disrupt various aspects of the internet.

For instance, it backed ElevenLabs, which many other startups use to integrate voice AI tools. It's also backed newer applications, such as Status, an AI gaming platform that lets people role-play as characters running a social media account.

Recent investments:

  • Status: an AI mobile gaming startup

FirstMark recently made its first bet in the dating space

Investor: Rick Heitzmann, cofounder and partner

Heitzmann is no stranger to consumer social. Since founding the firm in 2008, Heitzmann has led investments in social media companies like Pinterest and Discord.

FirstMark recently made its first investment in a dating app, backing ex-Hinge CEO Justin McLeod's new AI dating startup Overtone. The nascent dating app announced it raised $18 million in July.

"Probably a year ago, someone would've said 'dating,' I would've been like, 'Ugh, it's a horrible category,'" Heitzmann said. "But then you meet somebody who's thought about the world completely differently, more originally, and better than you have, and it changes your mind."

Overtone is one of many new consumer applications of AI that involve audio.

"We think that's definitely going to be, in the medium term, the primary way that people interface with AI and with applications," Heitzmann said.

Recent investments:

  • Posh: an IRL social feed of local events
  • Overtone: an AI dating app founded by Hinge's Justin McLeod

Forerunner has funded vibe-coding and dating apps

Investors: Kirsten Green, founding partner; Eurie Kim, managing partner; Fawzi Itani, principal

Before Kirsten Green founded Forerunner Ventures in 2012, she had never worked for a startup or VC firm.

Green started as an accountant and an equity research analyst. Forerunner Ventures has grown into an early-stage powerhouse, investing in companies like Glossier and Warby Parker. Green was featured on Business Insider's Seed 40 list for 2026, which highlights women early-stage investors.

Green continues to lead consumer investments, alongside Eurie Kim — who led Forerunner's Oura investment — and Fawzi Itani.

Recent investments:

  • Wabi: a platform for creating mini-apps without any coding
  • Known: an AI-powered dating service with audio matchmakers
  • Novig: a sports trading app for playing against other users

Goodwater backs Series A and growth-stage consumer startups

Investors: Chi-Hua Chien, cofounder and managing partner; Eric Kim, cofounder and managing partner; Ed Robinson, venture partner

Several years ago, Goodwater made a crucial early bet on Musical.ly — before it was acquired by ByteDance and merged with TikTok.

Goodwater has since made several investments in up-and-coming social startups, such as the events app Posh, the close-friends social network Yope, and the social calendar app Howbout. Per its website, Goodwater is looking for "the most promising consumer tech entrepreneurs."

The firm, based in the Bay Area, typically invests $5 million to $50 million into startups at the Series A and growth stages.

Recent investments:

  • Posh: an IRL social feed of local events
  • Kaon: a personalized AI entertainment platform

Alphabet's GV is making bets in AI content creation

Investor: Sangeen Zeb, general partner

Zeb is a general partner at GV (FKA Google Ventures) focused on early- and late-stage AI, enterprise, and consumer companies, including Harvey, Sierra, and OpenEvidence.

Before joining GV in 2021, Zeb was an investor with Founders Circle Capital, Centerview Capital, and Summit Partners.

Zeb said the best consumer founders have been tested early and are often "a study in contradictions." This includes unlikely career backgrounds — such as Dollhouse's Divanny Lamas, who worked in enterprise infrastructure before founding the AI video creation app — that allow them to see openings others miss.

Recent investments:

  • Cosmos: an image discovery platform for creatives seeking inspiration
  • Dollhouse Labs: an AI tool that creates a full production, from screenplay to finished video, which allows viewers to step in and make changes
  • Mother.tech: an AI creative studio that builds tools to generate images, videos, and memes

Khosla Ventures is looking for AI products that let people 'collaborate' and 'build relationships'

Investor: Adina Tecklu, partner

Tecklu joined Khosla in 2019 and is focused on early-stage software, AI, and fintech companies. Previously, she worked in seed investing at Canaan and was an early employee at Zenefits.

Tecklu said that the most compelling products "don't just help consumers accomplish tasks, they actively collaborate, create, entertain, or build relationships with them."

"They use conversational or multimodal interfaces, compress expertise into intuitive experiences, grow more personalized as they learn each person's preferences, and often unlock entirely new consumer behaviors," she said.

Recent investments:

  • Lovable: a vibe-coding platform that can build full-stack apps
  • Ollie: an AI assistant for parents that manages household tasks
  • Sekai: a platform that creates interactive mini-apps from text prompts
  • Gigi: a social network for professionals

Menlo Ventures turned 50 this year — and it's looking at the future of consumer

Investors: Shawn Carolan, partner; Amy Wu Martin, partner

In June, Menlo announced that the 50-year-old venture firm had raised $3 billion in capital to back the next generation of AI companies. The firm made a big bet on Anthropic and, with its fresh funding, is on the hunt for standout companies building something "net new," Carolan said.

Carolan joined the firm in 2002 and led its investments in Uber and Siri (pre-Apple acquisition). Martin joined Menlo in 2023 and was previously at Lightspeed Venture Partners and FTX Ventures. She's led Menlo's investments in AI companies like music startup Suno and social commerce tools like ShopMy and Alta.

One area Martin is eyeing is how startups are cracking multiplayer, multi-agent technologies that could help people better handle tasks or socialize. Carolan echoed this, adding that they're interested in seeing how AI could bring back truly social networks (which have largely become entertainment platforms), starting with the "reinvention of the contacts book."

Recent investments:

  • Suno: a music app for generating and sharing music with AI
  • Lemonade: a tool that lets people vibe-code Roblox games

Patron's investors have backgrounds in gaming and Discord

Investors: Amber Atherton, partner; Jason Yeh, cofounder and general partner; Brian Cho, cofounder and general partner

Patron's team is passionate about gaming and the ways that people connect — online and offline.

Atherton said the firm's approach to spotting promising consumer bets stems from keeping a close eye on "subcultures," especially in the AI era.

"Things that are starting to change on the edges could be a predictor around where mainstream consumer behavior is going to go," Atherton said. Wellness culture is one of those opportunities.

The early-stage firm has backed social startups like Arya, an AI relationship wellness platform, and Sweatpals, an app for finding IRL fitness and wellness classes and communities.

Recent investments:

  • Sweatpals: an IRL social fitness and wellness startup

Precursor has been around for over a decade and backs startups in the earliest stages

Investors: Charles Hudson, managing partner; Marina Girgis, principal

Hudson, based in San Francisco, founded Precursor more than a decade ago and has a knack for backing founders early on.

Girgis, based in New York, joined Precursor in 2021 after spending several years as an analyst at Bloomberg.

"We think a lot about the longer-term staying power of an app when we first invest," Girgis said. "What's the goal behind the app? How can you use the habits that you're creating or the data that you're collecting to create something really long-lasting?"

Last year, Precursor closed a $66 million fund (the generalist firm's fifth fund) to back early-stage startups. Precursor typically invests up to $500,000 in pre-seed or seed-stage startups.

Recent investments:

  • Lore: a fandom platform powered by AI search
  • Rec Technologies: a platform for local municipalities to coordinate IRL social experiences

Premise is a new firm focused on agentic tech at a consumer level

Investors: Vanessa Larco, cofounder and partner; Mercedes Bent, cofounder and partner

Larco and Bent teamed up last year to found Premise and are betting their careers on the potential for consumer internet startups in this AI era. The two investors previously held posts at NEA and Lightspeed, respectively.

Gaming — particularly Roblox — is one area Larco sees as an opportunity.

"Snapchat was for young millennials," she said. "Something like Roblox might be the next social network for Gen Alpha."

When it comes to AI, Larco said multiplayer, truly social, agentic tools have a long way to go before they're useful.

"The biggest problem right now in agent world is an agent has a really hard time knowing anyone exists beyond the person or the context it lives in," Larco said.

Recent investments:

  • Janitor AI: a platform for generating interactive stories using AI
  • Sitch: an AI dating startup

Note: Larco and Bent invested as angels

Alexis Ohanian's Seven Seven Six fund has backed music, AI, and dating apps

Investor: Alexis Ohanian

After cofounding Reddit, one of social media's biggest companies, Alexis Ohanian is looking smaller.

One year after leaving Reddit's board, Ohanian launched Seven Seven Six (776), his venture capital firm. 776 has consistently invested in consumer platforms, from music sharing to micro dramas and restaurant discovery. Ohanian backed a dating app, Left Field, when he starred as an investor on "Shark Tank."

In November, Ohanian told Business Insider that he was looking to replicate the potency of the group chat.

"This generation coming up has learned, and has a preference for, a less gamified version of life," he said. "They're choosing a healthier type of paradigm for social."

Recent investments:

  • Airbuds: an app for friends to share their music listening
  • GammaTime: a micro drama streaming platform for American audiences
  • Left Field: a dating app that matches based on shared interests and communities
  • Zest: an app using AI and purchase data to make restaurant recommendations

Shine Capital wants to bankroll the next gen of 'human and social experiences'

Investor: Ethan Daly, general partner

Six years ago, Daly was Shine Capital's first hire. Now, he's made partner and leads investments.

Daly told Business Insider that growth in consumer AI has been relegated primarily to productivity tools and NSFW applications. Meanwhile, "legacy social networks" are more focused on engagement than connecting people, he added.

"We believe this creates a massive opportunity," Daly said. "We're backing the next generation of human and social experiences."

He also cofounded Earshot, a studio hosting DJ sets, dinners, and art exhibitions.

Recent investments:

  • Cosmos: an image discovery platform for creatives seeking inspiration
  • Turnout: a consumer advocacy platform using AI to help navigate government and financial bureaucracies
  • Gizmo: an AI-powered social learning app that brings gamified mechanics to studying
  • [Untitled]: a platform for creating, editing, and sharing music

Spice Capital sees AI agents as the 'next consumer'

Investor: Maya Bakhai, founder and managing partner

Spice Capital launched in 2021 and focuses on early-stage companies that serve internet-native consumers across AI, blockchain, consumer, and deep tech. In recent months, the firm hired its first AI chief technology officer to automate internal operations and serve as a tech partner for its portfolio companies.

Consumers are becoming more "rational," Bakhai said, increasingly turning to AI assistants to help make purchasing decisions instead of relying on emotion or marketing. Over time, she expects startups will increasingly sell not to people, but to the AI agents making decisions on their behalf.

"The end state of that is that agents are the next consumer," she said.

Prior to Spice, Bakhai worked at Kevin Durant's VC firm Thirty Five Ventures.

Recent investments:

  • Aesthetic: an AI storefront that turns creator content into shoppable product recommendations
  • YKB: a social app for sports predictions

Stellation Capital is a New York-based fund founded by an ex-General Catalyst investor

Investors: Peter Boyce II, founder and managing partner; Rhian Horton, principal

A New York-based firm backing early-stage startups, Stellation was founded by Boyce II after he left General Catalyst.

It's a generalist fund, but Stellation has made several bets on consumer startups.

It backed Software Applications Incorporated, a desktop tool for LLMs, which OpenAI acquired. It has also backed companies like Koodos, which makes the social app Shelf, and Swsh, a social platform for sharing content from large events like music festivals.

Horton joined Stellation Capital in 2023.

Recent investments:

  • Swsh: A social app for live events

Verdict Capital is a new firm cofounded by Niko Bonatsos

Investor: Niko Bonatsos, cofounder and managing director

Bonatsos left his post at General Catalyst after 15 years to launch a new firm, called Verdict Capital, in 2026. The firm's mandate? To lead early-stage investments in "founders who are freaks in a good way" and "building in categories that don't have a name yet," Bonatsos said.

Bonatsos has a history in consumer. At GC, he invested in companies like Snap, Discord, and Triumph Arcade, as well as newer social startups, including Status and IRL-social startup 222.

Verdict has already invested in 15 companies, and 30% of those investments are in the consumer category, Bonatsos said.

He sees ample opportunity as an investor right now because of how AI is allowing some founders to "reimagine entire categories" and build "much faster."

Recent investments:

  • ForgeGUI: an AI game developer platform

Uncommon Projects is made up of several ex-Snapchat employees

Investors: Patrick Mandia, Eddie Koai, Ceci Mourkogiannis, Chad DePue, Antoine Martin

Uncommon Projects has backed several up-and-coming social startups, including close-friends social apps Locket and Retro, vibe-coding social app Gizmo, and professional networking startup Series. It also backed BeReal, as well as AI companies like Mirage (formerly Captions) and AI notetaker company Granola.

"We're a group of friends that met while working on product at Snapchat — we look for companies with an uncommon perspective on how people communicate and grow together," the firm's website says.

Per DePue's LinkedIn, Uncommon invests checks of $100,000 or more.

Some of the investors are also building startups in the space. Martin is the cofounder of Amo, the company behind Bump. DePue cofounded AI dating app Sitch.

Recent investments:

• Eigen: An AI social startup building a "mutual friend" to connect people

Previously, she worked in seed investing at Canaan and was an early employee at Zenefits.Tecklu said that the most compelling products "don't just help consumers accomplish tasks, they actively collaborate, create, entertain, or build relationships with them.""They use conversational or multimodal interfaces, compress expertise into intuitive experiences, grow more personalized as they learn each person's preferences, and often unlock entirely new consumer behaviors," she said.Recent investments:Lovable: a vibe-coding platform that can build full-stack appsOllie: an AI assistant for parents that manages household tasksSekai: a platform that creates interactive mini-apps from text promptsGigi: a social network for professionals Menlo Ventures turned 50 this year — and it's looking at the future of consumer Menlo Ventures partners Amy Wu Martin and Shawn Carolan. Menlo Ventures Investors: Shawn Carolan, partner; Amy Wu Martin, partnerIn June, Menlo announced that the 50-year-old venture firm had raised $3 billion in capital to back the next generation of AI companies. The firm made a big bet on Anthropic and, with its fresh funding, is on the hunt for standout companies building something "net new," Carolan said.Carolan joined the firm in 2002 and led its investments in Uber and Siri (pre-Apple acquisition). Martin joined Menlo in 2023 and was previously at Lightspeed Venture Partners and FTX Ventures. She's led Menlo's investments in AI companies like music startup Suno and social commerce tools like ShopMy and Alta.One area Martin is eyeing is how startups are cracking multiplayer, multi-agent technologies that could help people better handle tasks or socialize. Carolan echoed this, adding that they're interested in seeing how AI could bring back truly social networks (which have largely become entertainment platforms), starting with the "reinvention of the contacts book."Recent investments:Suno: a music app for generating and sharing music with AILemonade: a tool that lets people vibe-code Roblox games Patron's investors have backgrounds in gaming and Discord Patron's Brian Cho, Jason Yeh, and Amber Atherton. Courtesy of Patron Investors: Amber Atherton, partner; Jason Yeh, cofounder and general partner; Brian Cho, cofounder and general partnerPatron's team is passionate about gaming and the ways that people connect — online and offline.Atherton said the firm's approach to spotting promising consumer bets stems from keeping a close eye on "subcultures," especially in the AI era."Things that are starting to change on the edges could be a predictor around where mainstream consumer behavior is going to go," Atherton said. Wellness culture is one of those opportunities.The early-stage firm has backed social startups like Arya, an AI relationship wellness platform, and Sweatpals, an app for finding IRL fitness and wellness classes and communities.Recent investments:Sweatpals: an IRL social fitness and wellness startup Precursor has been around for over a decade and backs startups in the earliest stages Precursor's Charles Hudson and Marina Girgis. Courtesy of Precursor Investors: Charles Hudson, managing partner; Marina Girgis, principalHudson, based in San Francisco, founded Precursor more than a decade ago and has a knack for backing founders early on.Girgis, based in New York, joined Precursor in 2021 after spending several years as an analyst at Bloomberg."We think a lot about the longer-term staying power of an app when we first invest," Girgis said. "What's the goal behind the app? How can you use the habits that you're creating or the data that you're collecting to create something really long-lasting?"Last year, Precursor closed a $66 million fund (the generalist firm's fifth fund) to back early-stage startups. Precursor typically invests up to $500,000 in pre-seed or seed-stage startups.Recent investments:Lore: a fandom platform powered by AI searchRec Technologies: a platform for local municipalities to coordinate IRL social experiences Premise is a new firm focused on agentic tech at a consumer level Premise's Vanessa Larco and Mercedes Bent. Courtesy of Premise Investors: Vanessa Larco, cofounder and partner; Mercedes Bent, cofounder and partnerLarco and Bent teamed up last year to found Premise and are betting their careers on the potential for consumer internet startups in this AI era. The two investors previously held posts at NEA and Lightspeed, respectively.Gaming — particularly Roblox — is one area Larco sees as an opportunity."Snapchat was for young millennials," she said. "Something like Roblox might be the next social network for Gen Alpha."When it comes to AI, Larco said multiplayer, truly social, agentic tools have a long way to go before they're useful."The biggest problem right now in agent world is an agent has a really hard time knowing anyone exists beyond the person or the context it lives in," Larco said.Recent investments:Janitor AI: a platform for generating interactive stories using AISitch: an AI dating startupNote: Larco and Bent invested as angels Alexis Ohanian's Seven Seven Six fund has backed music, AI, and dating apps Alexis Ohanian. Eamonn M. McCormack/Getty Images for SXSW London Investor: Alexis OhanianAfter cofounding Reddit, one of social media's biggest companies, Alexis Ohanian is looking smaller.One year after leaving Reddit's board, Ohanian launched Seven Seven Six (776), his venture capital firm. 776 has consistently invested in consumer platforms, from music sharing to micro dramas and restaurant discovery. Ohanian backed a dating app, Left Field, when he starred as an investor on "Shark Tank."In November, Ohanian told Business Insider that he was looking to replicate the potency of the group chat."This generation coming up has learned, and has a preference for, a less gamified version of life," he said. "They're choosing a healthier type of paradigm for social."Recent investments:Airbuds: an app for friends to share their music listeningGammaTime: a micro drama streaming platform for American audiencesLeft Field: a dating app that matches based on shared interests and communitiesZest: an app using AI and purchase data to make restaurant recommendations Shine Capital wants to bankroll the next gen of 'human and social experiences' Shine Capital's Ethan Daly. Brian Dorsey Studios Investor: Ethan Daly, general partnerSix years ago, Daly was Shine Capital's first hire. Now, he's made partner and leads investments.Daly told Business Insider that growth in consumer AI has been relegated primarily to productivity tools and NSFW applications. Meanwhile, "legacy social networks" are more focused on engagement than connecting people, he added."We believe this creates a massive opportunity," Daly said. "We're backing the next generation of human and social experiences."He also cofounded Earshot, a studio hosting DJ sets, dinners, and art exhibitions.Recent investments:Cosmos: an image discovery platform for creatives seeking inspirationTurnout: a consumer advocacy platform using AI to help navigate government and financial bureaucraciesGizmo: an AI-powered social learning app that brings gamified mechanics to studying[Untitled]: a platform for creating, editing, and sharing music Spice Capital sees AI agents as the 'next consumer' Spice Capital's Maya Bakhai. Dilpreet Shah Investor: Maya Bakhai, founder and managing partnerSpice Capital launched in 2021 and focuses on early-stage companies that serve internet-native consumers across AI, blockchain, consumer, and deep tech. In recent months, the firm hired its first AI chief technology officer to automate internal operations and serve as a tech partner for its portfolio companies.Consumers are becoming more "rational," Bakhai said, increasingly turning to AI assistants to help make purchasing decisions instead of relying on emotion or marketing. Over time, she expects startups will increasingly sell not to people, but to the AI agents making decisions on their behalf."The end state of that is that agents are the next consumer," she said.Prior to Spice, Bakhai worked at Kevin Durant's VC firm Thirty Five Ventures.Recent investments:Aesthetic: an AI storefront that turns creator content into shoppable product recommendationsYKB: a social app for sports predictions Stellation Capital is a New York-based fund founded by an ex-General Catalyst investor Stellation Capital's Peter Boyce II and Rhian Horton. Courtesy of Stellation Capital Investors: Peter Boyce II, founder and managing partner; Rhian Horton, principalA New York-based firm backing early-stage startups, Stellation was founded by Boyce II after he left General Catalyst.It's a generalist fund, but Stellation has made several bets on consumer startups.It backed Software Applications Incorporated, a desktop tool for LLMs, which OpenAI acquired. It has also backed companies like Koodos, which makes the social app Shelf, and Swsh, a social platform for sharing content from large events like music festivals.Horton joined Stellation Capital in 2023.Recent investments:Swsh: A social app for live events Verdict Capital is a new firm cofounded by Niko Bonatsos Niko Bonatsos cofounded Verdict Capital. Kimberly White/Getty Images for TechCrunch Investor: Niko Bonatsos, cofounder and managing directorBonatsos left his post at General Catalyst after 15 years to launch a new firm, called Verdict Capital, in 2026. The firm's mandate? To lead early-stage investments in "founders who are freaks in a good way" and "building in categories that don't have a name yet," Bonatsos said.Bonatsos has a history in consumer. At GC, he invested in companies like Snap, Discord, and Triumph Arcade, as well as newer social startups, including Status and IRL-social startup 222.Verdict has already invested in 15 companies, and 30% of those investments are in the consumer category, Bonatsos said.He sees ample opportunity as an investor right now because of how AI is allowing some founders to "reimagine entire categories" and build "much faster."Recent investments:ForgeGUI: an AI game developer platform Uncommon Projects is made up of several ex-Snapchat employees Illustration by Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images Investors: Patrick Mandia, Eddie Koai, Ceci Mourkogiannis, Chad DePue, Antoine MartinUncommon Projects has backed several up-and-coming social startups, including close-friends social apps Locket and Retro, vibe-coding social app Gizmo, and professional networking startup Series. It also backed BeReal, as well as AI companies like Mirage (formerly Captions) and AI notetaker company Granola."We're a group of friends that met while working on product at Snapchat — we look for companies with an uncommon perspective on how people communicate and grow together," the firm's website says.Per DePue's LinkedIn, Uncommon invests checks of $100,000 or more.Some of the investors are also building startups in the space. Martin is the cofounder of Amo, the company behind Bump. DePue cofounded AI dating app Sitch.Recent investments:• Eigen: An AI social startup building a "mutual friend" to connect people Read next Sydney Bradley You're currently following this author! sentences.en: Something has to change, right?

blocks.html_en: Instagram, Snapchat, and TikTok have dominated the social media landscape for years. As AI infiltrates every platform — and many corners of our lives — new social media founders, apps, and internet behaviors are emerging.

sentences.en: Instagram, Snapchat, and TikTok have dominated the social media landscape for years.
sentences.zh: Instagram、Snapchat 和 TikTok 多年来一直主导着社交媒体格局。

sentences.en: As AI infiltrates every platform — and many corners of our lives — new social media founders, apps, and internet behaviors are emerging.
sentences.zh: 随着人工智能渗透到每个平台以及我们生活的许多角落，新的社交媒体创始人、应用程序和互联网行为不断涌现。

blocks.html_en: From vibe coding to cultural shifts shaping how people want to connect, it's a perfect storm for founders to experiment with social media.

sentences.en: From vibe coding to cultural shifts shaping how people want to connect, it's a perfect storm for founders to experiment with social media.
sentences.zh: 从氛围编码到塑造人们想要联系的方式的文化转变，对于创始人来说，尝试社交媒体是一场完美的风暴。

blocks.html_en: AI has "essentially brought down the cost of making anything," Amy Wu Martin, a partner at Menlo Ventures, told Business Insider.

sentences.en: AI has "essentially brought down the cost of making anything," Amy Wu Martin, a partner at Menlo Ventures, told Business Insider.
sentences.zh: Menlo Ventures 合伙人艾米·吴·马丁 (Amy Wu Martin) 告诉《商业内幕》，人工智能“从根本上降低了制造任何东西的成本”。

blocks.html_en: Menlo is an investor in Anthropic, whose product Claude Code has radically changed the product-building pipeline for startups.

sentences.en: Menlo is an investor in Anthropic, whose product Claude Code has radically changed the product-building pipeline for startups.
sentences.zh: Menlo 是 Anthropic 的投资者，其产品 Claude Code 从根本上改变了初创公司的产品构建流程。

blocks.html_en: "If you want to build a consumer AI company, you may not need a lot of engineers, but you still need cash for Anthropic credits," said Vanessa Larco, who cofounded Premise, a new VC firm backing AI-native startups.

sentences.en: "If you want to build a consumer AI company, you may not need a lot of engineers, but you still need cash for Anthropic credits," said Vanessa Larco, who cofounded Premise, a new VC firm backing AI-native startups.
sentences.zh: “如果你想建立一家消费者人工智能公司，你可能不需要很多工程师，但你仍然需要现金来换取人类学分，”Vanessa Larco 说，她是 Premise 的联合创始人，Premise 是一家支持人工智能本土初创公司的新风险投资公司。

blocks.html_en: While it's easier than ever to make an app out of thin air, it's difficult to raise venture capital for a consumer-facing social media upstart.

sentences.en: While it's easier than ever to make an app out of thin air, it's difficult to raise venture capital for a consumer-facing social media upstart.
sentences.zh: 虽然凭空制作应用程序比以往任何时候都容易，但为面向消费者的社交媒体新贵筹集风险资本却很困难。

blocks.html_en: PitchBook data on social startup investments shows that after a peak in 2021 (239 deals totaling $2.2 billion), the number of deals has declined each year. In 2025, PitchBook counted 97 deals totaling $330 million.

sentences.en: PitchBook data on social startup investments shows that after a peak in 2021 (239 deals totaling $2.2 billion), the number of deals has declined each year.
sentences.zh: PitchBook 的社交创业投资数据显示，在 2021 年达到峰值（239 笔交易，总计 22 亿美元）之后，交易数量逐年下降。

sentences.en: In 2025, PitchBook counted 97 deals totaling $330 million.
sentences.zh: 2025 年，PitchBook 统计了 97 笔交易，总价值 3.3 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: Still, there's a cohort of investors with hungry eyes searching for what will stick.

sentences.en: Still, there's a cohort of investors with hungry eyes searching for what will stick.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，仍有一群投资者热切地寻找能够坚持下去的东西。

blocks.html_en: Business Insider is highlighting investors at 22 VC firms that are actively funding or scouting out innovative social startups. Our third annual list is sourced from nominations by startup founders and venture peers, as well as our own reporting.

sentences.en: Business Insider is highlighting investors at 22 VC firms that are actively funding or scouting out innovative social startups.
sentences.zh: Business Insider 重点介绍了 22 家风投公司的投资者，这些公司正在积极资助或寻找创新型社会初创企业。

sentences.en: Our third annual list is sourced from nominations by startup founders and venture peers, as well as our own reporting.
sentences.zh: 我们的第三份年度榜单来自初创公司创始人和风险同行的提名以及我们自己的报告。

blocks.html_en: These investors, across the board, are excited by what AI has unlocked for the category, and many are bullish on consumer investments even as the broader VC space shies away from it. Our list includes big-shot firms that backed many of the mobile-era giants, as well as AI-era funds founded by VCs who wanted to take things into their own hands.

sentences.en: These investors, across the board, are excited by what AI has unlocked for the category, and many are bullish on consumer investments even as the broader VC space shies away from it.
sentences.zh: 这些投资者普遍对人工智能为该类别带来的成果感到兴奋，许多人看好消费者投资，尽管更广泛的风险投资领域回避它。

sentences.en: Our list includes big-shot firms that backed many of the mobile-era giants, as well as AI-era funds founded by VCs who wanted to take things into their own hands.
sentences.zh: 我们的名单包括支持许多移动时代巨头的大公司，以及由希望将事情掌握在自己手中的风险投资公司创立的人工智能时代基金。

blocks.html_en: They're backing startups building agentic solutions for our personal relationships, companies focused on getting us off our phones and into real life, and vibe-coding startups turning software into content.

sentences.en: They're backing startups building agentic solutions for our personal relationships, companies focused on getting us off our phones and into real life, and vibe-coding startups turning software into content.
sentences.zh: 他们支持为我们的人际关系构建代理解决方案的初创公司、专注于让我们摆脱手机进入现实生活的公司，以及将软件转化为内容的振动编码初创公司。

blocks.html_en: As Nicole Quinn — a former investor at Lightspeed Venture Partners who left in 2025 to cofound Connect Ventures — put it, funding this next wave of internet startups will largely be investing in the "future of joy."

sentences.en: As Nicole Quinn — a former investor at Lightspeed Venture Partners who left in 2025 to cofound Connect Ventures — put it, funding this next wave of internet startups will largely be investing in the "future of joy."
sentences.zh: 正如 Lightspeed Venture Partners 前投资者 Nicole Quinn 于 2025 年离职并共同创立 Connect Ventures 所说，为下一波互联网初创公司提供资金将主要投资于“快乐的未来”。

blocks.html_en: "The end state of that is that agents are the next consumer," she said.

有些事情必须改变，对吧？

Instagram、Snapchat 和 TikTok 多年来一直主导着社交媒体格局。 随着人工智能渗透到每个平台以及我们生活的许多角落，新的社交媒体创始人、应用程序和互联网行为不断涌现。

从氛围编码到塑造人们想要联系的方式的文化转变，对于创始人来说，尝试社交媒体是一场完美的风暴。

Menlo Ventures 合伙人艾米·吴·马丁 (Amy Wu Martin) 告诉《商业内幕》，人工智能“从根本上降低了制造任何东西的成本”。

Menlo 是 Anthropic 的投资者，其产品 Claude Code 从根本上改变了初创公司的产品构建流程。

“如果你想建立一家消费者人工智能公司，你可能不需要很多工程师，但你仍然需要现金来换取人类学分，”Vanessa Larco 说，她是 Premise 的联合创始人，Premise 是一家支持人工智能本土初创公司的新风险投资公司。

虽然凭空制作应用程序比以往任何时候都容易，但为面向消费者的社交媒体新贵筹集风险资本却很困难。

PitchBook 的社交创业投资数据显示，在 2021 年达到峰值（239 笔交易，总计 22 亿美元）之后，交易数量逐年下降。 2025 年，PitchBook 统计了 97 笔交易，总价值 3.3 亿美元。

尽管如此，仍有一群投资者热切地寻找能够坚持下去的东西。

Business Insider 重点介绍了 22 家风投公司的投资者，这些公司正在积极资助或寻找创新型社会初创企业。 我们的第三份年度榜单来自初创公司创始人和风险同行的提名以及我们自己的报告。

这些投资者普遍对人工智能为该类别带来的成果感到兴奋，许多人看好消费者投资，尽管更广泛的风险投资领域回避它。 我们的名单包括支持许多移动时代巨头的大公司，以及由希望将事情掌握在自己手中的风险投资公司创立的人工智能时代基金。

他们支持为我们的人际关系构建代理解决方案的初创公司、专注于让我们摆脱手机进入现实生活的公司，以及将软件转化为内容的振动编码初创公司。

正如 Lightspeed Venture Partners 前投资者 Nicole Quinn 于 2025 年离职并共同创立 Connect Ventures 所说，为下一波互联网初创公司提供资金将主要投资于“快乐的未来”。

与 22 家风险投资公司的投资者见面，寻找能够改变我们在线联系方式的初创公司：

A16z 拥有支持许多早期互联网初创公司的加速器

投资者：Andrew Chen，普通合伙人；奥利维亚·摩尔，合伙人；贾斯汀·摩尔，合伙人

Andreessen Horowitz 支持移动时代许多最热门的产品，包括 Instagram、Pinterest、Reddit 和 Roblox。 它还支持了 2020 年代的许多社交公司，例如 BeReal、Clubhouse、Partiful 和 Substack。

该公司一直积极支持人工智能的消费者应用，投资者包括双胞胎投资者奥利维亚和贾斯汀·摩尔。

A16z 还运营着一家具有竞争力的加速器 Speedrun，该加速器向早期消费初创公司投资高达 100 万美元。 该加速器由普通合伙人安德鲁·陈 (Andrew Chen) 牵头。

近期投资：

  • Town：用于管理收件箱和会议的人工智能代理服务
  • Sekai：根据文本提示创建交互式迷你应用程序的平台
  • Sitch：一款人工智能驱动的约会应用程序
  • Wabi：无需任何编码即可创建迷你应用程序的平台

巴比伦正在关注对文化有明显影响的新初创企业

投资者：Hugo Amsellem，联合创始人兼普通合伙人

Amsellem 是一位法国投资者，最近搬到了旧金山。 2026 年，他将公司从 Intuition VC 更名为 Babylon，将基金重点投资于“推动文化”的初创企业。

在成为风险投资家之前，他曾在创作者经济初创公司 Jellysmack 工作，负责监督并购策略。 Babylon 投资了一系列处于早期阶段的消费者社交初创公司，包括音乐社交应用 Airbuds、meme 和 GIF 平台 Klipy 以及人工智能社交初创公司 Eigen。

他正在关注那些在人工智能驱动的社交网络上建立新模式的初创公司，比如 Eigen。 这家社交初创公司正在为人工智能构建一个“共同朋友”层，这意味着一种将你与人们联系起来并分享信息的方式，就像现实生活中的朋友一样。

阿姆塞勒姆说：“这就是人工智能将实现的事情，”它增强了“那些现有的人类行为，而无需真正的人类介入。”

Babylon 通常会为种子前轮开出高达 250,000 美元的支票。

近期投资：

  • Eigen：一家人工智能社交初创公司，建立“共同的朋友”来联系人们
  • Miso：一款代理旅行应用程序
  • 德尔福：人工智能孪生初创公司

Creator Ventures 名字中带有“creator”字样，但它加倍投资消费互联网初创公司

投资者：Sasha Kaletsky，联合创始人兼执行合伙人

Kaletsky 与英国 YouTuber Caspar Lee 共同创立了 Creator Ventures，目前居住在纽约。 该公司专注于早期消费互联网初创公司，于 2025 年以 4500 万美元的价格完成了第二期基金的募集。

Creator Ventures 投资了那些希望颠覆互联网各个方面的初创公司。

例如，它支持了 ElevenLabs，许多其他初创公司都使用该公司来集成语音人工智能工具。 它还支持更新的应用程序，例如 Status，这是一个人工智能游戏平台，可以让人们扮演运行社交媒体帐户的角色。

近期投资：

  • 现状：人工智能移动游戏初创公司

FirstMark 最近在约会领域下了第一笔赌注

投资者：Rick Heitzmann，联合创始人兼合伙人

海茨曼对消费者社交并不陌生。 自 2008 年创立公司以来，Heitzmann 主导了对 Pinterest 和 Discord 等社交媒体公司的投资。

FirstMark 最近对约会应用程序进行了第一笔投资，支持前 Hinge 首席执行官 Justin McLeod 的新人工智能约会初创公司 Overtone。 这款新生的约会应用程序宣布 7 月份筹集了 1800 万美元。

“可能一年前，有人会说‘约会’，我会说，‘呃，这是一个可怕的类别，’”海茨曼说。 “但当你遇到一个对世界的看法完全不同、更原创、比你更好的人时，你的想法就会改变。”

Overtone 是众多涉及音频的人工智能新消费应用之一。

海茨曼说：“我们认为，从中期来看，这肯定会成为人们与人工智能和应用程序交互的主要方式。”

近期投资：

  • Posh：当地活动的 IRL 社交源
  • Overtone：由 Hinge 的 Justin McLeod 创立的人工智能约会应用程序

先行者资助了氛围编码和约会应用程序

投资人：Kirsten Green，创始合伙人；尤里·金 (Eurie Kim)，管理合伙人；法齐·伊塔尼，校长

在 Kirsten Green 于 2012 年创立 Forerunner Ventures 之前，她从未在初创公司或风险投资公司工作过。

格林最初是一名会计师和股票研究分析师。 Forerunner Ventures 已发展成为一家早期投资公司，投资了 Glossier 和 Warby Parker 等公司。 Green 入选 Business Insider 2026 年 Seed 40 名单，该名单重点关注女性早期投资者。

Green 继续领导消费者投资，与领导 Forerunner 的 Oura 投资的 Eurie Kim 和 Fawzi Itani 一起。

近期投资：

  • Wabi：无需任何编码即可创建迷你应用程序的平台
  • 已知：人工智能驱动的约会服务与音频媒人
  • Novig：一款用于与其他用户对战的体育交易应用程序

Goodwater 支持 A 轮融资和成长阶段的消费初创公司

投资人：Chi-Hua Chien，联合创始人兼管理合伙人； Eric Kim，联合创始人兼执行合伙人；埃德·罗宾逊，风险合伙人

几年前，古德沃特在 Musical.ly 被字节跳动收购并与 TikTok 合并之前对其进行了至关重要的早期投资。

此后，古德沃特对崭露头角的社交初创公司进行了多项投资，例如活动应用程序 Posh、好友社交网络 Yope 和社交日历应用程序 Howbout。 根据其网站，古德沃特正在寻找“最有前途的消费科技企业家”。

该公司总部位于湾区，通常向 A 轮融资和成长期的初创公司投资 500 万至 5000 万美元。

近期投资：

  • Posh：当地活动的 IRL 社交源
  • Kaon：个性化AI娱乐平台

Alphabet 的 GV 正在押注人工智能内容创作

投资者：Sangeen Zeb，普通合伙人

Zeb 是 GV (FKA Google Ventures) 的普通合伙人，专注于早期和后期人工智能、企业和消费者公司，包括 Harvey、Sierra 和 OpenEvidence。

在 2021 年加入 GV 之前，Zeb 是 Founders Circle Capital、Centerview Capital 和 Summit Partners 的投资者。

泽布表示，最好的消费者创始人很早就接受了考验，而且往往是“矛盾中的研究”。 这包括一些不太可能的职业背景——比如 Dollhouse 的 Divanny Lamas，他在创立人工智能视频创作应用程序之前曾在企业基础设施部门工作——这让他们能够看到其他人错过的机会。

近期投资：

  • Cosmos：为创意人员寻找灵感的图像发现平台
  • Dollhouse Labs：一种人工智能工具，可以创建从剧本到成品视频的完整作品，允许观众介入并进行更改
  • Mother.tech：人工智能创意工作室，构建生成图像、视频和模因的工具

Khosla Ventures 正在寻找能让人们“协作”和“建立关系”的人工智能产品

投资人：Adina Tecklu，合伙人

Tecklu 于 2019 年加入 Khosla，专注于早期软件、人工智能和金融科技公司。 此前，她曾在 Canaan 从事种子投资工作，并且是 Zenefits 的早期员工。

特克卢表示，最引人注目的产品“不仅帮助消费者完成任务，还能积极地与消费者协作、创造、娱乐或建立关系。”

她说：“他们使用对话式或多模式界面，将专业知识压缩为直观体验，在了解每个人的偏好时变得更加个性化，并且常常会解锁全新的消费者行为。”

近期投资：

  • Lovable：一个可以构建全栈应用程序的氛围编码平台
  • Ollie：为父母管理家务的人工智能助手
  • Sekai：一个根据文本提示创建交互式迷你应用程序的平台
  • Gigi：专业人士的社交网络

Menlo Ventures 今年已成立 50 周年——它正在着眼于消费者的未来

投资人：Shawn Carolan，合伙人；吴艾米·马丁，合伙人

6 月，Menlo 宣布这家拥有 50 年历史的风险投资公司已筹集 30 亿美元资金，以支持下一代人工智能公司。 卡罗兰说，该公司在 Anthropic 上下了很大的赌注，并利用新的资金，寻找杰出的公司来打造“全新的”东西。

Carolan 于 2002 年加入该公司，领导了对 Uber 和 Siri（苹果收购前）的投资。 Martin 于 2023 年加入 Menlo，此前曾在 Lightspeed Venture Partners 和 FTX Ventures 工作。 她领导了 Menlo 对音乐初创公司 Suno 等人工智能公司以及 ShopMy 和 Alta 等社交商务工具的投资。

马丁关注的一个领域是初创公司如何破解多人、多代理技术，以帮助人们更好地处理任务或社交。 卡罗兰对此表示赞同，并补充说，他们有兴趣了解人工智能如何从“通讯录的重塑”开始，带回真正的社交网络（社交网络在很大程度上已成为娱乐平台）。

近期投资：

  • Suno：一款利用人工智能生成和分享音乐的音乐应用程序
  • Lemonade：一种可以让人们对 Roblox 游戏进行氛围编码的工具

Patron 的投资者拥有游戏和 Discord 背景

投资人：Amber Atherton，合伙人； Jason Yeh，联合创始人兼普通合伙人； Brian Cho，联合创始人兼普通合伙人

Patron 的团队对游戏和人们在线和离线的联系方式充满热情。

阿瑟顿表示，该公司发现有前途的消费者赌注的方法源于密切关注“亚文化”，尤其是在人工智能时代。

阿瑟顿说：“边缘开始发生的变化可能是主流消费者行为走向的预测。” 健康文化就是其中之一。

这家早期公司支持了 Arya（一个人工智能关系健康平台）和 Sweatpals（一款用于寻找 IRL 健身和健康课程和社区的应用程序）等社交初创公司。

近期投资：

  • Sweatpals：一家现实生活中的社交健身和健康初创公司

Precursor 已存在十多年，并在最早阶段为初创公司提供支持

投资者：Charles Hudson，执行合伙人；玛丽娜·吉尔吉斯，校长

Hudson 总部位于旧金山，十多年前创立了 Precursor，并且拥有早期支持创始人的诀窍。

Girgis 居住在纽约，在彭博社担任了几年分析师后，于 2021 年加入 Precursor。

“当我们第一次投资时，我们会考虑很多应用程序的长期持久力，”吉尔吉斯说。 “该应用程序背后的目标是什么？ 你如何利用你正在养成的习惯或你正在收集的数据来创造真正持久的东西？”

去年，Precursor 关闭了一只 6600 万美元的基金（这家综合型公司的第五只基金）来支持早期初创企业。 Precursor 通常向种子前或种子阶段初创公司投资高达 500,000 美元。

近期投资：

  • Lore：一个由人工智能搜索驱动的粉丝平台
  • Rec Technologies：地方市政当局协调 IRL 社交体验的平台

Premise 是一家专注于消费者层面代理技术的新公司

投资人：Vanessa Larco，联合创始人兼合伙人；梅赛德斯·本特 (Mercedes Bent)，联合创始人兼合伙人

Larco 和 Bent 去年联手创立了 Premise，并将自己的职业生涯押注于这个人工智能时代消费互联网初创公司的潜力。 这两位投资者此前分别在 NEA 和 Lightspeed 任职。

游戏——尤其是 Roblox——被 Larco 视为一个机会领域。

“Snapchat 是为年轻的千禧一代设计的，”她说。 “像 Roblox 这样的东西可能会成为 Gen Alpha 的下一个社交网络。”

谈到人工智能，拉科表示，多人游戏、真正的社交、代理工具在发挥作用之前还有很长的路要走。

拉科说：“目前特工世界中最大的问题是，特工很难知道除该人或其所生活的环境之外还有其他人存在。”

近期投资：

  • Janitor AI：使用人工智能生成互动故事的平台
  • Sitch：人工智能约会初创公司

注：Larco 和 Bent 作为天使投资

Alexis Ohanian 的七七六基金支持音乐、人工智能和约会应用程序

投资者：亚历克西斯·奥哈尼安

在共同创立社交媒体最大公司之一 Reddit 后，亚历克西斯·奥哈尼安 (Alexis Ohanian) 的规模似乎越来越小。

离开 Reddit 董事会一年后，奥哈尼安创立了他的风险投资公司七七六 (776)。 776持续投资消费平台，从音乐分享到微剧和餐厅发现。 奥哈尼安 (Ohanian) 在《创智赢家》(Shark Tank) 中担任投资人时，支持了一款约会应用程序 Left Field。

11 月，奥哈尼安告诉《商业内幕》，他正在寻求复制群聊的效力。

“这一代人已经学会并且偏爱一种不那么游戏化的生活，”他说。 “他们正在选择一种更健康的社交模式。”

近期投资：

  • Airbuds：一款供朋友分享音乐聆听的应用程序
  • GammaTime：面向美国观众的微剧流媒体平台
  • Left Field：一款根据共同兴趣和社区进行匹配的约会应用程序
  • Zest：一款使用人工智能和购买数据来推荐餐厅的应用程序

Shine Capital 希望为下一代“人类和社会体验”提供资金

投资者：Ethan Daly，普通合伙人

六年前，Daly 是 Shine Capital 的第一位员工。 现在，他已成为合伙人并领导投资。

Daly 告诉 Business Insider，消费者人工智能的增长主要集中在生产力工具和 NSFW 应用程序上。 与此同时，他补充说，“传统社交网络”更注重参与而不是联系人们。

“我们相信这创造了巨大的机会，”戴利说。 “我们支持下一代人类和社会体验。”

他还共同创立了 Earshot，这是一家举办 DJ 表演、晚宴和艺术展览的工作室。

近期投资：

  • Cosmos：为创意人员寻找灵感的图像发现平台
  • Turnout：一个使用人工智能来帮助管理政府和金融官僚机构的消费者倡导平台
  • Gizmo：一款人工智能驱动的社交学习应用程序，将游戏化机制带入学习中
  • [Untitled]：创作、编辑和分享音乐的平台

Spice Capital 将人工智能代理视为“下一个消费者”

投资者：Maya Bakhai，创始人兼管理合伙人

Spice Capital 于 2021 年成立，专注于为人工智能、区块链、消费者和深度技术领域的互联网原生消费者提供服务的早期公司。 近几个月来，该公司聘请了第一位人工智能首席技术官，以实现内部运营自动化，并作为其投资组合公司的技术合作伙伴。

巴哈伊说，消费者变得更加“理性”，越来越多地求助于人工智能助手来帮助做出购买决定，而不是依赖情感或营销。 随着时间的推移，她预计初创公司将越来越多地不再向人们销售产品，而是向代表他们做出决策的人工智能代理销售产品。

“最终的结果是代理商成为下一个消费者，”她说。

在加入 Spice 之前，Bakhai 曾在凯文·杜兰特 (Kevin Durant) 的风险投资公司 Thirty Five Ventures 工作。

近期投资：

  • Aesthetic：人工智能店面，可将创作者内容转化为可购物的产品推荐
  • YKB：体育预测社交应用

Stellaration Capital 是一家总部位于纽约的基金，由前 General Catalyst 投资者创立

投资者：Peter Boyce II，创始人兼执行合伙人；瑞安·霍顿，校长

Stellation 是一家总部位于纽约的公司，为早期初创公司提供支持，由博伊斯二世 (Boyce II) 在离开 General Catalyst 后创立。

这是一只多面手基金，但 Sttellation 对消费类初创公司进行了多次押注。

它支持 Software Applications Incorporated，这是一款针对法学硕士的桌面工具，已被 OpenAI 收购。 它还支持了 Koodos 和 Swsh 等公司，Koodos 开发了社交应用 Shelf，Swsh 是一个用于分享音乐节等大型活动内容的社交平台。

霍顿于 2023 年加入星群资本。

近期投资：

  • Swsh：用于现场活动的社交应用程序

Verdict Capital 是一家由 Niko Bonatsos 共同创立的新公司

投资者：Niko Bonatsos，联合创始人兼董事总经理

Bonatsos 在 General Catalyst 任职 15 年后辞去职务，于 2026 年成立了一家名为 Verdict Capital 的新公司。 公司的授权？ 博纳索斯说，领导对“以良好方式表现出怪胎的创始人”和“建立尚未命名的类别”的早期投资。

Bonatsos 在消费者领域有着悠久的历史。 在 GC，他投资了 Snap、Discord 和 Triumph Arcade 等公司，以及较新的社交初创公司，包括 Status 和 IRL 社交初创公司 222。

Bonatsos 表示，Verdict 已经投资了 15 家公司，其中 30% 投资于消费领域。

他认为目前作为投资者有充足的机会，因为人工智能让一些创始人能够“重新构想整个类别”并“更快地”构建。

近期投资：

  • ForgeGUI：人工智能游戏开发者平台

Uncommon Projects 由几位前 Snapchat 员工组成

投资者：Patrick Mandia、Eddie Koai、Ceci Mourkogiannis、Chad DePue、Antoine Martin

Uncommon Projects 已支持多家新兴社交初创公司，包括好友社交应用程序 Locket 和 Retro、振动编码社交应用程序 Gizmo 以及专业网络初创公司 Series。 它还支持 BeReal 以及 Mirage（以前称为 Captions）和人工智能笔记公司 Granola 等人工智能公司。

该公司网站称：“我们是一群在 Snapchat 开发产品时认识的朋友，我们寻找对人们如何沟通和共同成长拥有不同寻常视角的公司。”

根据 DePue 的 LinkedIn，Uncommon 投资了 10 万美元或更多的支票。

一些投资者也在该领域建立初创公司。 Martin 是 Bump 背后的公司 Amo 的联合创始人。 DePue 是人工智能约会应用程序 Sitch 的联合创始人。

近期投资：

• Eigen：一家人工智能社交初创公司，建立“共同的朋友”来联系人们
